verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family of proteins. Unlike IZUMO1 (a single-pass TM protein critical for sperm-egg fusion), IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is predicted to be a secreted/soluble protein. It contains an Ig-like domain but no GPI-signal or TM anchor. It is expressed predominantly in testis and other tissues but shows no documented stable PM surface localization. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no evidence of stress-induced surface display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — reproductive tissue expression but no TM anchor to support surface display; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — no evidence; (4) dual_localization — no TM precursor described; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no wash-resistant anchoring to a TM partner documented. No antibody/ADC/CAR-T programs targeting IZUMO4 on cell surfaces are known. The protein body is not accessible from the extracellular face of the plasma membrane.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 is poorly characterized; secreted vs. intracellular localization is not definitively established by surface proteomics or flow on intact cells. A minor PM pool or TM isoform cannot be fully excluded.
